skill.name: code-refactoring-refactor-clean
skill.description: Guides safe code refactoring with clean code principles, SOLID design, incremental plans, tests, and maintainability checks.
skill.summary: Refactor code safely with clean architecture, focused tests, and clear before-and-after quality goals.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, filesystem, reconnaissance, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found these are markdown examples and ordinary refactoring terminology. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, executable scripts, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md or resources/implementation-playbook.md.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives from markdown code fences, language examples, and resource-file references. They do not instruct the agent to run shell commands or execute untrusted input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited SKILL.md lines only reference the playbook, and the playbook locations are fenced examples about refactoring patterns. No command runner, dynamic execution instruction, or user-input execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Network and Filesystem Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The network and filesystem hits appear in a legacy-code example that the playbook presents as code to refactor away. The content does not direct the agent to send email, open log files, or access hidden files during skill execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The email and file-write snippets are inside a before example for decomposing a monolith. The regex lines were misclassified as hidden-file access because of escaped dot characters, not actual filesystem access.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography and reconnaissance hits are false positives from words such as SOLID, dependency metrics, checklist text, and ordinary class or method examples. No cryptographic function, scanning workflow, or reconnaissance instruction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found no MD5, SHA1, cryptographic API use, host scanning, or network discovery behavior. The cited text is refactoring guidance and quality checklist content.

content.user_title: Refactor Code Safely With Clean Architecture
content.value_statement: Complex code becomes risky when changes are large, untested, or poorly scoped. This skill guides incremental refactoring with clean design and verification steps.
actual_capabilities: Identifies code smells, duplication, long methods, large classes, and coupling risks.
Builds prioritized refactoring plans with effort, risk, and expected impact.
Applies SOLID principles through practical before-and-after refactoring patterns.
Recommends tests, coverage checks, and regression verification for changed behavior.
Provides migration notes when refactors affect public interfaces or dependencies.
Summarizes quality metrics such as complexity, method size, and coverage changes.
limitations: It depends on the code and context supplied by the user.
It cannot guarantee behavior preservation without reliable tests or runtime validation.
It may need human approval before architecture changes or breaking migrations.
It provides guidance and examples, not a standalone automated refactoring engine.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Legacy Modules
use_cases.description: Break large methods and tangled classes into smaller units while keeping behavior stable.

use_cases.title: Prepare Code for New Features
use_cases.description: Reduce coupling and improve naming before adding feature work to fragile areas.

use_cases.title: Improve Review Readiness
use_cases.description: Create a scoped refactor plan with test updates, risk notes, and measurable quality goals.

prompt_templates.title: Find Simple Refactor Targets
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small file
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this file for simple refactoring opportunities. Focus on naming, duplication, long functions, and behavior-preserving improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Incremental Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning a safe refactor before editing
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this module and create an ordered refactoring plan. Include risk level, expected impact, and tests needed for each step.

prompt_templates.title: Apply SOLID Improvements
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this code using SOLID principles where they clearly reduce complexity. Explain each design change and keep the public behavior stable.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor With Migration Guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this component and provide migration notes for callers. Include compatibility concerns, test updates, and before-and-after quality metrics.

output_examples.input: A service class handles validation, persistence, email notifications, and logging in one long method.
output: Analysis summary with the mixed responsibilities and main risk areas.
Refactor plan that extracts validation, repository, notification, and logging responsibilities.
Verification notes covering unit tests, integration tests, and behavior checks.

output_examples.input: A team wants to reduce technical debt before adding a new feature to a legacy module.
output: Prioritized list of low-risk cleanup steps before feature work.
Design recommendations for reducing coupling and improving testability.
Migration guidance if public interfaces need small changes.

output_examples.input: A pull request contains a broad refactor with limited explanation.
output_examples.output: A concise review summary that separates behavior changes, pure cleanup, missing tests, and follow-up risks.

best_practices: Start with tests or characterization checks before changing fragile code.
Refactor in small slices with clear before-and-after behavior expectations.
Measure results with complexity, coverage, duplication, and review feedback.
anti_patterns: Combining refactoring with unrelated feature changes in one large diff.
Changing public behavior without documenting migration impact or approval.
Applying design patterns where simple functions or names would solve the problem.

faq.question: Does this skill change code automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide code changes when used in an editing tool, but it is mainly a refactoring workflow and playbook.

faq.question: Which languages does it support?
faq.answer: The playbook includes examples in Python, TypeScript, Java, Go, JavaScript, and configuration formats.

faq.question: Can it help with architecture refactoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers modular design, dependency inversion, repositories, service boundaries, and migration planning.

faq.question: How does it reduce regression risk?
faq.answer: It emphasizes incremental changes, test updates, behavior checks, and explicit migration notes.

faq.question: Is it suitable for small fixes?
faq.answer: It is most useful for meaningful cleanup. A one-line fix usually does not need this skill.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: It includes security-aware refactoring checks, hardcoded secret detection guidance, and static analysis recommendations.
